pathways.label: Dismissal and not-guilty expunction under G.S. 15A-146
pathways.summary: Three main families, considered by disposition. Family 1 — Dismissals and not-guilty findings (G.S. 15A-146) The broadest and easiest relief: Dismissed charges (by the State or the court) — a single dismissal or multiple dismissals may be expunged; the court shall order expunction on finding the charge(s) were dismissed. Not-guilty / not-responsible findings — expungeable once all related charges reach final disposition.
pathways.sourceRef: section:73-116; pathways:73-116; corpus-unit:28; section:51-72; section:117-157; section:241-272; section:308-333; section:334-365
ruleClauses: Three main families, considered by disposition.
Family 1 — Dismissals and not-guilty findings (G.S. 15A-146)
The broadest and easiest relief:
Dismissed charges (by the State or the court) — a single dismissal or multiple dismissals may be expunged; the court shall order expunction on finding the charge(s) were dismissed.
Not-guilty / not-responsible findings — expungeable once all related charges reach final disposition.
No bars: 15A-146 has NO prior-conviction bar and NO prior-expunction bar — a person may use it even with other convictions or earlier expunctions.

Automatic expunction (on/after Dec 1, 2021): if ALL charges in a case are dismissed or result in not-guilty on/after 12/1/2021, the case is expunged BY OPERATION OF LAW 180–210 days after final disposition — no petition (with limited exceptions, e.g., certain dismissals via deferred prosecution/conditional discharge).
Family 2 — Nonviolent conviction expunctions (G.S. 15A-145.5)
The Second Chance Act conviction relief, limited to “nonviolent” offenses:
One nonviolent misdemeanor — after 3 years (reduced from 5 by the 2025 law, for petitions filed on/after 7/9/2025).
More than one nonviolent misdemeanor — after 7 years from the last conviction / completion of sentence.
One nonviolent felony — after 10 years from completion of the sentence.
Two or three nonviolent felonies — after 20 years; all nonviolent felonies disposed in the same session of court count as one.
Requires no disqualifying convictions, a good-character showing, no outstanding restitution, and (for most) no prior 15A-145.5 expunction.
Family 3 — Youthful / pre-Raise-the-Age expunctions (G.S. 15A-145.8A) and others

15A-145.8A: convictions for Class H/I felonies and misdemeanors committed at age 16 or 17 BEFORE December 1, 2019 may be expunged by petition (or by the district attorney) after the sentence is completed, with no outstanding restitution; a judge must grant an eligible petition.

Other targeted statutes: under-18 / under-21 offenses (15A-145), gang offenses before 18 (15A-145.1), certain drug conditional discharges (15A-145.2 / 145.3), and a nonviolent felony before 18 (15A-145.4) — each with its own form and rules.
Route by disposition first. Dismissal or not-guilty → 15A-146 (no wait, no priors bar; often automatic).
Conviction → classify as nonviolent and use 15A-145.5 with the waiting tier. Old teen conviction → check
For any case dismissed/acquitted on or after 12/1/2021, check whether AUTOMATIC expunction already
applies before preparing a petition — the person may not need to file.

sourceSections.title: Eligibility rules
sourceSections.text:
Three main families, considered by disposition.

Family 1 — Dismissals and not-guilty findings (G.S. 15A-146)
The broadest and easiest relief:

• Dismissed charges (by the State or the court) — a single dismissal or multiple dismissals may be expunged;
the court shall order expunction on finding the charge(s) were dismissed.
• Not-guilty / not-responsible findings — expungeable once all related charges reach final disposition.
• No bars: 15A-146 has NO prior-conviction bar and NO prior-expunction bar — a person may use it even
with other convictions or earlier expunctions.
• Automatic expunction (on/after Dec 1, 2021): if ALL charges in a case are dismissed or result in not-guilty
on/after 12/1/2021, the case is expunged BY OPERATION OF LAW 180–210 days after final disposition —
no petition (with limited exceptions, e.g., certain dismissals via deferred prosecution/conditional discharge).

Family 2 — Nonviolent conviction expunctions (G.S. 15A-145.5)
The Second Chance Act conviction relief, limited to “nonviolent” offenses:

• One nonviolent misdemeanor — after 3 years (reduced from 5 by the 2025 law, for petitions filed on/after
7/9/2025).
• More than one nonviolent misdemeanor — after 7 years from the last conviction / completion of sentence.
• One nonviolent felony — after 10 years from completion of the sentence.
• Two or three nonviolent felonies — after 20 years; all nonviolent felonies disposed in the same session of
court count as one.
• Requires no disqualifying convictions, a good-character showing, no outstanding restitution, and (for most)
no prior 15A-145.5 expunction.

Family 3 — Youthful / pre-Raise-the-Age expunctions (G.S. 15A-145.8A) and others
• 15A-145.8A: convictions for Class H/I felonies and misdemeanors committed at age 16 or 17 BEFORE
December 1, 2019 may be expunged by petition (or by the district attorney) after the sentence is completed,
with no outstanding restitution; a judge must grant an eligible petition.
• Other targeted statutes: under-18 / under-21 offenses (15A-145), gang offenses before 18 (15A-145.1),
certain drug conditional discharges (15A-145.2 / 145.3), and a nonviolent felony before 18 (15A-145.4) —
each with its own form and rules.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Route by disposition first. Dismissal or not-guilty → 15A-146 (no wait, no priors bar; often automatic).
Conviction → classify as nonviolent and use 15A-145.5 with the waiting tier. Old teen conviction → check
15A-145.8A.
For any case dismissed/acquitted on or after 12/1/2021, check whether AUTOMATIC expunction already
applies before preparing a petition — the person may not need to file.

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

sourceSections.text:
For conviction expunctions, eligibility turns on whether the offense is “nonviolent” as defined in G.S.
15A-145.5(a). It is defined by exclusion.

NOT “nonviolent” (categorically ineligible for 15A-145.5)
• Any Class A through G felony (only Class H and I felonies can qualify as nonviolent).
• Any Class A1 misdemeanor (the most serious misdemeanor class).
• Impaired driving (DWI) and related offenses under G.S. 20-138.1 et seq.
• Sex offenses and any offense requiring sex-offender registration.
• Offenses involving assault as an essential element, certain offenses against minors, stalking, and other
enumerated crimes.
• Offenses involving methamphetamine, certain controlled-substance trafficking, and hate crimes, as
enumerated.

• Dismissals and not-guilty findings under 15A-146 have NO nonviolent limitation, NO prior-conviction bar,
and NO prior-expunction bar — any dismissed charge (with narrow exceptions) is expungeable.
• Out-of-state and federal convictions are not expungeable under North Carolina law, but they count when
assessing eligibility for conviction expunctions.

VERIFY BEFORE RELYING

AGENT GUIDANCE
First screen: is the conviction a Class A–G felony, a Class A1 misdemeanor, a DWI, or a sex offense? If
yes, it's not “nonviolent” and 15A-145.5 can't reach it.
DWI is the classic trap — impaired-driving convictions are excluded from the nonviolent definition and
cannot be expunged as convictions.

sourceSections.title: Case outcome rules
sourceSections.text:
Disposition and class drive the statute, the wait, and whether a filing is needed. This table is the agent's primary
routing logic for North Carolina.

Case outcome Statute Route Wait / note

Dismissed (state or court) 15A-146 Petition; often automatic None

Not guilty / not responsible 15A-146 Petition; often automatic None

All charges dismissed/NG 15A-146 Automatic, 180–210 days No filing
(on/after 12/1/2021)

One nonviolent misdemeanor 15A-145.5 Petition (AOC-CR-298) 3 yrs

Multiple nonviolent misd. 15A-145.5 Petition (AOC-CR-298) 7 yrs

One nonviolent felony 15A-145.5 Petition (AOC-CR-297) 10 yrs

Two–three nonviolent felonies 15A-145.5 Petition (AOC-CR-297) 20 yrs

Conviction at 16–17 pre-12/2019 15A-145.8A Petition (AOC-CR-296) On completion

Class A–G felony / A1 misd. / — Not eligible (convictions) Categorical
DWI / sex offense

Effect of relief (G.S. 15A-153): once expunged, the person is restored to the status occupied before the arrest,
charge, or conviction — and may lawfully deny that it occurred, including on most employment and licensing
applications. After an expunction, the clerk of superior court has NO record of the case and cannot provide
documentation (even the expunction order is destroyed with the file), so the petitioner should keep their
certified copy. The relief reaches North Carolina records only.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Tell users the expunction is strong — they can deny the matter — but warn that the court destroys the
file, including the order, so they must keep the certified copy they receive.
For dismissals/acquittals on/after 12/1/2021, confirm whether automatic expunction already applied
(180–210 days out) before filing anything.

sourceSections.title: Required forms (AOC-CR)
sourceSections.text:
North Carolina uses statute-specific Administrative Office of the Courts forms (the AOC-CR series), filed in the
Superior Court of the county where the charge was brought.

Form Use

AOC-CR-287 Petition & order to expunge a DISMISSAL (G.S. 15A-146(a)/(a1))

AOC-CR-288 Petition & order to expunge an ACQUITTAL / not-guilty (15A-146(a2))

AOC-CR-298 Expunge ONE or MORE nonviolent MISDEMEANOR convictions (15A-145.5)

AOC-CR-297 Expunge nonviolent FELONY conviction(s) (15A-145.5)

AOC-CR-296 Youthful convictions at 16–17 before 12/1/2019 (15A-145.8A)

AOC-CR-264 etc. Other targeted statutes (under-18/21, drug conditional discharge, etc.)

Each petition form contains the petition, the affidavit/consent to record checks, and the order for the judge. For
conviction expunctions, the petitioner consents to two record checks — the AOC checks for prior expunctions
and the DPS State Bureau of Investigation (SBI) checks for prior convictions — and the form includes
affidavits of good character. For 15A-146 dismissals, neither a prior expunction nor a prior record check is a
bar, and the AOC forms do not require service on the prosecutor.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Pick the form by statute: CR-287 (dismissal), CR-288 (acquittal), CR-298 (nonviolent misdemeanor),
CR-297 (nonviolent felony), CR-296 (youthful). Using the wrong form is a common rejection reason.
For conviction petitions, build in the AOC + SBI record-check consents and the good-character affidavits;
for dismissals, those checks aren't required.

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

sourceSections.title: Filing instructions
sourceSections.text:
1 Get the record. Obtain the petitioner's North Carolina criminal record (the SBI / DPS criminal history) and
the court file to confirm each charge, disposition, offense class, and sentence-completion dates.

2 Confirm the statute and eligibility. Dismissal/acquittal (15A-146) vs. nonviolent conviction (15A-145.5)
vs. youthful (15A-145.8A); the nonviolent classification; the waiting period; the no-other-conviction and
prior-expunction rules; and any outstanding restitution.

3 Complete the matching AOC-CR form (CR-287/288/297/298/296), including the record-check consents
and good-character affidavits for conviction petitions.
4 File in the Superior Court of the county where the charge was brought, and pay the $175 filing fee (waived
for true dismissals/acquittals; charged where a charge was dismissed via deferred prosecution / conditional
discharge, and for conviction petitions). A fee waiver may apply for indigent petitioners (e.g.,
SNAP/TANF/SSI or legal-services representation).

5 Record checks / DA review. For conviction petitions, the AOC and SBI complete their checks and the
district attorney is given notice and may be heard; the AOC forms for dismissals do not require service on the
prosecutor.

6 Hearing or no-hearing order. A judge may grant a 15A-146 dismissal expunction without a hearing;
conviction petitions are decided after the record checks and any DA input.

7 Order and destruction. If granted, the order directs the SBI, DPS, DMV, AOC, the clerk, and the arresting
agency to expunge the records. The clerk then has no record of the case — keep the certified copy of the
order.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Venue is the Superior Court of the county where the charge was brought. The $175 fee applies to
conviction petitions and to deferred/conditional dismissals, but true dismissals and acquittals are free —
and indigent petitioners may qualify for a waiver.
Warn the petitioner up front that after expunction the court keeps NO copy — they must safeguard the
certified order they receive.

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

sourceSections.title: Court-specific quirks
sourceSections.text:
• Statute-specific forms. NC has a separate AOC-CR form for nearly every expunction pathway; the right
statute drives the right form.
• Automatic dismissal expunction. Cases fully dismissed or resulting in not-guilty on/after 12/1/2021 are
expunged by operation of law 180–210 days after final disposition — no petition.
• 2025 misdemeanor change. One nonviolent misdemeanor now waits 3 years (down from 5) for petitions
filed on/after 7/9/2025; AOC-CR-298 (July 2025) reflects it.
• “Nonviolent” is defined by exclusion. Class A–G felonies, Class A1 misdemeanors, DWI, and sex offenses
are out; only Class H/I felonies and lower misdemeanors can qualify.
• Same-session rule. Nonviolent felonies disposed in one session of court count as a single felony for the count
and the waiting tier.
• Two record checks. Conviction petitions require AOC (prior expunctions) and SBI (prior convictions)
checks; 15A-146 dismissals do not.
• Court keeps no copy. After expunction the clerk destroys the file, including the order — the petitioner's
certified copy is the only proof.
• $175 fee, with carve-outs. Conviction petitions and deferred/conditional dismissals cost $175; true
dismissals and acquittals are free; indigent waivers exist.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Always confirm the offense class and the matching AOC-CR form before drafting — NC's per-statute
form system is unforgiving of the wrong form.
Check automatic-expunction timing for recent dismissals, and the 2025 three-year misdemeanor wait,
before telling a user they must wait or file.

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

sourceSections.title: Fee information
sourceSections.text:
Item Amount Note

Conviction expunction (15A-145.5) $175 Per petition

Youthful expunction (15A-145.8A) $175 Per petition (DA-filed may differ)

Dismissal via deferred prosecution / $175 Fee applies
conditional discharge

True dismissal / not guilty (15A-146) $0 No fee; often automatic

Indigent fee waiver $0 SNAP/TANF/SSI or legal-services representation

SBI / AOC record checks Included Part of the conviction-petition process

Attorney fees (if represented) Varies Not required; Second Chance Alliance & clinics assist

Most expunction petitions carry a $175 filing fee, but the picture is friendlier than it looks. A charge that was
simply dismissed or resulted in a not-guilty finding is FREE to expunge (and is often expunged automatically),
with the $175 fee reserved for dismissals that came through a deferred-prosecution or conditional-discharge
agreement and for conviction petitions. Indigent petitioners — including those receiving SNAP, TANF, or SSI,
or represented by a legal-services organization — may qualify for a fee waiver. The NC Second Chance
Alliance and court self-help resources assist for free.

AGENT GUIDANCE
Lead with the free routes: true dismissals and acquittals cost nothing and are often automatic. The $175
fee is for conviction petitions and deferred/conditional dismissals.
Screen for a fee waiver — SNAP/TANF/SSI or legal-services representation can zero out the $175.

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

sourceSections.title: Criminal history (SBI / AOC) request process
sourceSections.text:
Confirming the record first establishes the offense class (which decides the nonviolent classification and tier),
the dispositions, and the sentence-completion dates.

sourceSections.title: Sample petitions and completed examples
sourceSections.text:
North Carolina expunctions are filed on the matching AOC-CR form in Superior Court. The examples below
show structure and key recitals. Names, dates, and identifiers are fictional and illustrative.

Example A — Expunge One Nonviolent Misdemeanor (AOC-CR-298, G.S. 15A-145.5)
STATE OF NORTH CAROLINA, COUNTY OF WAKE
In the General Court of Justice, Superior Court Division
STATE v. ANDRE L. CARTER File No. 19CR012345

PETITION AND ORDER OF EXPUNCTION (G.S. 15A-145.5)
1. Petitioner was convicted of the nonviolent misdemeanor listed
on Side One (Larceny, Class 1 misdemeanor, G.S. 14-72).
2. The offense is 'nonviolent' under 15A-145.5 (not a Class A-G
felony, Class A1 misdemeanor, DWI, or sex offense).
3. Petitioner was convicted of, and completed any active,
probationary, or post-release sentence for, the offense at
least THREE years before filing (2025 one-misdemeanor wait).
4. Petitioner has no other conviction (other than a traffic
offense) at any time, and none during the waiting period.
5. Petitioner has not previously received a 15A-145.5 expunction,
has no outstanding restitution, and is of good character.
6. Petitioner consents to the AOC (prior-expunction) and SBI
(prior-conviction) record checks.
ORDER: the SBI, DPS, DMV, AOC, the clerk, and the arresting
agency shall expunge all records of this conviction. Fee: $175.
(After expunction the clerk retains no record; keep the
certified copy.)

Examples B, C & D — Other pathways

sourceSections.title: YOUTHFUL (15A-145.8A, AOC-CR-296):
sourceSections.text:
Class H/I felonies and misdemeanors committed at 16-17 before
12/1/2019 (pre-Raise-the-Age) may be expunged after the
sentence is completed with no outstanding restitution; a judge
must grant an eligible petition. The DA may also file.

Petition and Order

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma

Each AOC-CR form combines the petition, the petitioner's affidavits and consents, and the order for the judge.
On a granted order, the named agencies (SBI, DPS, DMV, AOC, the clerk of superior court, and any arresting
agency) expunge the records; under G.S. 15A-153 the matter is deemed not to have occurred and the petitioner
may deny it. Because the clerk destroys the file (including the order), the certified copy mailed to the petitioner
is the only documentation.

This reference is an internal training and retrieval aid for the Wilma assistant. It summarizes North Carolina's expunction statutes
amended it in 2025 (S.L. 2025-71, reducing the one-misdemeanor wait to 3 years); dismissal expunctions are often automatic. The
agent must verify the current text of Ch. 15A (§§ 15A-145.5, 15A-146, 15A-145.8A), the “nonviolent” definition, the matching
AOC-CR forms, SBI/AOC processes, and current fees before relying on any rule here.

North Carolina Expunction — Agent Training Reference · LegalEase / Wilma
